author: Nicole Fende
description: Have you lost touch with your creativity somewhere along the way? Been told you're "not the creative type"? Hosted by Nicole Fende and Monette Satterfield, each episode is a judgment-free zone where we explore creativity as a birthright, not a talent reserved for a lucky few. title: Aging Athletically with Alison Heilig
author: Alison Heilig
description:
Women have long been told: At midlife, it's time to slow down. Be gentle. Watch from the sidelines.
But this advice is only accelerating women's decline.
What if instead of accepting decline, we built our strongest body in midlife that makes us faster, stronger, and more efficient than ever before?
Women in midlife are presented with a unique set of biological challenges, but that doesn't mean we need to accept defeat. It just means we need a better strategy.
Aging Athletically is for the woman who wants the world to stay open to her: whether that's running your 20th ultra or just loading your suitcase into the overhead bin without assistance.
I'm Alison Heilig: Running Coach, Corrective Exercise Specialist, Certified Nutrition Coach, Women's Fitness Specialist, Yoga Medicine® Registered Therapeutic Specialist, and author of The Durable Runner. 
And I believe your strongest years are ahead of you. Let's go.

You started making music because you love creating. Not because you wanted to spend your days buried in emails, scheduling, spreadsheets, admin tasks, and endless to-do lists.

But as an independent musician, you’re expected to do it all.

The music.

The marketing.

The planning.

The logistics.

The business.

And it can quickly become overwhelming.

Welcome to The Organized Musician, the podcast for independent musicians who want to build sustainable music careers without burning out.

I’m Kate, a Virtual Assistant for Indie Musicians. Each week I’ll help you simplify the business side of music through organization, outsourcing, systems, and conversations with industry professionals.

Inside the show, you’ll learn how to:

🎵 Stay organized as an independent musician

🎵 Decide what to outsource and when

🎵 Manage the business side of your music career

🎵 Build sustainable workflows and systems

🎵 Reclaim time for creating music

🎵 Learn from musicians, music industry professionals, and service providers

This isn’t about doing more.

It’s about creating a music career that actually supports your creativity instead of competing with it. If you’re ready to spend less time overwhelmed by admin and more time making music, you’re in the right place.

Welcome to The Organized Musician.

🎶 Ready to spend less time on admin and more time making music? Schedule a free discovery call: www.themusicianassistant.com

✉️ kate@themusicianassistant.com

title: St. Augustine of Hippo:  Expositions of the Psalms
author: Mary McDonald
description: This podcast presents themes from Expositions of the Psalms, the last work of Augustine that is what Michael Fiedrowicz says is his “mature theology.”  This set of six volumes is not well known because of its tremendous length and its strange literary form.  Augustine went line by line to explicate poetry, and he parked these explanations into homilies.  Nonetheless, fragments of this warm, witty, and profound text were found in monasteries all over Europe at the fall of Rome, influencing clergy greatly, and thus in large part inspiring the spread of Western Christianity.  These podcasts offer viewers facets of this work to enhance their understanding of Augustine’s magnificent vision of creation, his synthesis of the roles of Christ, and his direction for the interior development needed to live a Christian life.  To delve into this work yourself, find all six volumes at New City Press:  https://www.focolaremedia.com/bookstore/6-volume-expositions-psalms-set-study-edition

title: Sister to Sister Podcast
author: AFRICAN WOMEN'S RIGHTS ADVOCATES
description:
Sister to Sister is a podcast by African Women's Rights Advocates (AWRA) and a flagship product of the Sister to Sister Initiative, a platform dedicated to strengthening solidarity, learning, and collective action among women leaders and advocates across Africa.

The podcast amplifies the voices, knowledge, and lived experiences of grassroots activists, survivors, feminists, researchers, policymakers, and movement leaders working to advance the rights of women and girls. Through bold and honest conversations, each episode explores critical issues including ending female genital mutilation (FGM), child marriage, gender-based violence, feminist leadership, movement building, and the realities of activism.

Sister to Sister is a space for dialogue, healing, reflection, and shared learning. By centering the experiences of women at the forefront of social change, it celebrates resilience, challenges harmful social norms, and inspires collective action toward a future where every woman and girl can live free from violence, discrimination, and inequality.

title: First Responder Reset | Sleep Deprivation, Fatigue, Burnout, Trauma, Recovery
author: Amie Peterson - First Responder Counselor & Owner of Thin Line 206
description:
Can’t shut your brain off after shift? Still replaying calls when your head hits the pillow? Tired but wired? Burned out, emotionally drained, and wondering why you can’t relax even when you’re finally home?
Maybe you’re still showing up. Still doing the job. Still taking care of everyone else.
But your sleep is falling apart. Your fuse is shorter. Your family gets what’s left of you. The calls still follow you home, and your nervous system never seems to get the message that the danger is over.
You’re not weak.
You’re not broken.
And you’re DEFINITELY not the only one.
Welcome to First Responder Reset—the podcast for law enforcement, firefighters, EMS, dispatchers, corrections, Search & Rescue, healthcare professionals, military veterans, and other high-stress professionals who are tired of living in survival mode.
Each episode is built around practical, tactical, and culturally competent strategies to help you recover from burnout, cumulative trauma, operational stress, hypervigilance, and the critical incidents that stick with you. We’ll talk about nervous system regulation, sleep and recovery, work-life balance, relationships, resilience, and how to leave work, at work, without losing the edge that makes you good at what you do.
Hey, I’m Amie—a licensed trauma therapist, Certified EMDR Therapist, daughter of a Police Chief, and the product of a multigenerational law enforcement family. For nearly a decade, I’ve worked almost exclusively with first responders, military and combat veterans, healthcare professionals, and other high-stress occupations.
I’ve sat across from the detective carrying child abuse and ICAC cases long after the evidence was logged. The firefighter running on little sleep and too many bad calls. The medic who can’t stop replaying a pediatric scene. The dispatcher whose brain never slows down. The corrections officer who never fully lets their guard down. The Search & Rescue volunteer carrying difficult recoveries. The combat veteran who spent years mission ready & deployed and can’t figure out how to turn it off. And the spouses and families who just want their responder back.
For years, I watched good people tell themselves:
”I’m fine.”
”It just comes with the job.”
”I can handle it.”
”I just need more sleep.”
 
But here’s what I’ve learned.
 
Most people in this profession aren’t looking for mental health.
They’re looking for relief.
Relief from poor sleep.
Relief from survival mode.
Relief from hypervigilance.
Relief from burnout.
Relief from carrying the job home.
Relief from feeling like they must do it alone.
If you’re ready to stop carrying the weight of the job by yourself, improve sleep, reconnect with your family, understand what’s happening inside your nervous system, and get your life back, you’re in the right place.
So, finish that report, throw on the headphones after shift, and let’s get to work.

title: BACH 52
author: Nicholas Phan
description:
Is Johann Sebastian Bach's music really for everyone?


BACH 52 is a web series and podcast hosted by Grammy-winning tenor Nicholas Phan, exploring that question one cantata at a time. Each episode pairs a performance of a Bach aria with a conversation between Nick and a guest from the worlds of music, medicine, theology, education, and beyond — people who bring unexpected perspectives to some of the most profound music ever written.


Bach composed over 200 sacred cantatas for the Lutheran church. BACH 52 is on a journey through that music — asking what this deeply Christian music means for believers and non-believers alike, and why it continues to resonate across cultures, faiths, and centuries.


Guests have included musicians, conductors, physicians, educators, clergy, and industry leaders — all united by a lifelong relationship with Bach's music and a willingness to explore what it means for the world we live in today.


Start anywhere — every episode stands alone.


Learn more: https://www.bach52.nicholas-phan.com/

title: Changing Course Gracefully
author: Elaina Kelly Smith
description:
Have you ever had that quiet, heavy feeling that you’re just a passenger in your own life? On paper, everything looks complete—you have the career, the stability, and the responsibilities of a capable adult. But inside, there is a nagging disconnection, a low hum of moving through your days on autopilot. I call this "Remote Control Living."

Welcome to Changing Course Gracefully. Hosted by author Elaina Kelly Smith, this show is an open invitation to step off autopilot, put down the invisible rules holding you back, and begin a profound, healing conversation with yourself. Through raw personal stories from all seven continents and the practical, gentle tools of the PARQS framework—Preferences, Awareness, Right Action, Questions, and Self-Acceptance—you’ll learn how to lay down your "fixer" dictionary, listen to your own body's feedback loop, and step-by-step build a durable foundation of deep self-trust. You don’t have to become someone new. You just have to return to who you have been all along. Welcome home to yourself.

title: The Frequency Fix
author: John D. Jr., LMSW
description:
Deep Dives
The Story Behind the Feeling.
Every song tells a story.
Sometimes, it tells part of yours.

Hosted by John, a Licensed Social Worker and educator, Deep Dives uses music as the doorway into larger conversations about psychology, identity, relationships, culture, communication, and the universal experiences that shape us. We explore ideas through evidence-informed research and thoughtful storytelling; we'll explore the questions great music leaves behind—not to diagnose people or explain artists, but to understand ourselves better. This isn't a music review show. It's not celebrity analysis. And it's not therapy. 

It's an invitation to slow down, think deeply, and discover what music can teach us about the human experience. Every episode concludes with The Deep Fix™, our signature reflection segment featuring companion songs, books, educational resources, journal prompts, and practical ways to continue the conversation long after the music fades.

Whether you're listening on your commute, during a late-night drive, or while taking a quiet walk, Deep Dives invites you to become more curious about the stories we tell ourselves.

New episodes every Thursday.

The Soundtrack for Self-Awareness.

The Frequency Fix™ is a music and self-awareness podcast hosted by John, LMSW, where we explore the stories, emotions, and life experiences hidden inside the songs people love most.

Each episode uses a song as a starting point for a deeper conversation about themes such as:

Relationships & Connection
Personal Growth
Identity
Boundaries & Self-Respect
Grief & Change
Resilience
Emotional Awareness
Life Transitions
Self-Reflection


Educational Disclaimer
Deep Dives is produced for educational and informational purposes only. This podcast explores music, psychology, relationships, and human behavior through an educational lens. It is not therapy, counseling, diagnosis, treatment, crisis intervention, or individualized mental health advice. Listening to this podcast does not establish a social worker-client relationship of any kind. If you're in the United States and experiencing a mental health crisis, call or text 988 or your emergency services. If you're seeking ongoing support, please connect with a licensed mental health professional in your area.

title: The Intimacy Series
author: Schuyler Emerick
description:
The Intimacy Series is a warm, plain-spoken guide to real closeness, hosted by Schuyler. Short, self-contained 12–15 minute episodes on the actual skills of connection — communication, trust, desire, presence, and more — written for listeners of every orientation and relationship. Educational, sex-positive, and non-explicit. New episodes weekly. Start with Episode 1.
About the host — Schuyler was born in the 1960s, a child of the flower-child generation, raised by liberal parents who taught him that openness beats judgment. He went to art school, found his way into finance, came out as gay in the 1980s, and in 1990 settled into a quiet suburban life in Florida. Then September 11th shook everything loose — life's too short — and he started actually living it: bolder, more curious, more himself. The gatherings he hosted grew into something bigger — sex-positive parties built on consent, warmth, and zero shame. A friendship with a longtime patron of Bob's Adult Books in Salem, Oregon deepened into a business partnership, and when his friend passed, Schuyler stepped into ownership of the shop. He's always been the type to ask — over the years he's built a deep bench of trusted advisors and experts, and he leans on them heavily to make this show, because he's the first to admit he doesn't know it all. As his father, a librarian, taught him: you don't need to know everything, you just need to know the people who do. The Intimacy Series is the sum of that whole journey — generous, grounded, and unafraid to talk about what most people won't.

title: Real Support for Real Healing
author: Markus Gibs
description: Clearview Conversations is a compassionate mental health podcast created around the values of person-centered care, practical education, and emotional support. Inspired by the approach of Clearview Behavioral Health and Wellness Services PLLC, this series explores the connection between mental health, daily life, and long-term wellbeing through accessible conversations that feel supportive rather than clinical. Clearview’s website emphasizes a dynamic, person-centered approach to psychopharmacological treatment, comprehensive psychiatric care, and the importance of listening carefully to each person’s goals and lived experience.Each episode is designed to reduce stigma, encourage hope, and help listeners better understand common mental health challenges, treatment options, and healthy coping tools. The series also reflects the welcoming tone of Clearview Behavioral Health and Wellness Services PLLC, where care is framed around understanding the individual, supporting mental wellbeing, and making it easier for people to take the next step toward help. The clinic’s site also notes that appointments may be made in person or via telemedicine, which reinforces the accessibility and flexibility behind the brand.In the middle of the series narrative, the podcast naturally introduces Clearview Behavioral Health and Wellness Services PLLC as a trusted place to learn more, connect with professional support, and take action toward healing. The overall objective is simple: to create a warm, informative audio resource that meets people where they are and gently guides them toward care, clarity, and confidence.

title: Dare to Listen
author: namehmarsin
description:
Dare to Listen is a space for conversations about self-discovery, transformation, and our connections with the unseen — the mystical, the ancestral, and the deeply personal inner worlds we each navigate.
Creator and host Nameh, a self-awareness coach and Human Design guide, believes that hearing someone else’s lived experience is one of the most powerful routes to self-understanding. Beyond the theory. Beyond the framework. Into the real, embodied, sometimes messy story of how a person came to know themselves more honestly — and how that knowing changed everything.
Each episode invites guests to share how they’ve moved through times of transformation — whether that’s through Human Design, past lives, inner child work, family constellations, death doula work, sobriety,  ancestral healing, and more. Some guests will be practitioners and guides bringing their technical wisdom and lived experience with a specific healing modality. Others will be people living their life with rich stories and wisdom to share about navigating what it means to be human.
Dare to Listen originally ran from 2019–2021 and is now relaunching in 2026 (in video form on YouTube) with Human Design woven in as a new layer. Some conversations will be steeped in HD. Others might barely mention HD. The episode titles will let you know what you’re stepping into.
Whether you’re brand new here or you’ve been listening since the beginning — I’m sooooo glad you’re here. Here’s invitation: dare to listen to what stirs in you as you hear these conversations.
Nameh began her Human Design deep dive in 2021 — completing IHDS foundational courses, hundreds of hours of self-study, non-IHDS analyst training, and continues experimenting with HD knowledge. A 3/5 Emotional Generator on the Cross of Explanation with split definition, she brings technical HD deets together with intuitive, embodied coaching work.

description:

Built around the quality-first philosophy of Purist Peptides, the show explores the importance of purity standards, batch verification, secure handling, and the role of research-use-only compounds in a responsible scientific environment.Each episode breaks down a different topic with a clear, educational lens covering research trends, quality control, product categories, storage considerations, and the mindset behind reliable sourcing.

Whether the conversation is about recovery-focused compounds, sleep and mood research, metabolic pathways, or longevity discussions, Purist Peptides serves as the trusted reference point for uncompromising standards and science-driven thinking.The objective of the series is simple: to make complex research topics easier to understand while reinforcing the value of consistency, precision, and verified quality. With a calm, informative tone and practical insights throughout, the podcast helps audiences engage with the research landscape in a smarter, more confident way.

title: AI.Proof PODCASTS

author: Chantal Hewitt - PDA Autism Support & Low Demand Parenting
description:
Is every day a battle you didn’t sign up for? You’re not failing. Your child isn’t broken. The approach just needs to change.

The Attuned Spectrum Podcast is for parents navigating the complex, exhausting, and often isolating reality of raising a PDA (Pathological Demand Avoidance) autistic child — whether they’re five, fifteen, or twenty-five.

Hosted by Chantal Hewitt — Family Autism Support Coach, experienced educator, late-diagnosed autistic, ADHD and PDA mum of three neurodivergent children including a PDA son — this is the podcast that meets you where you actually are. Not where you’re supposed to be.

Here we move beyond behaviour management and into nervous system safety, low-demand parenting, and connection-first approaches that actually work. We cover school refusal, autistic meltdowns, co-regulation, PDA burnout, and the transition to adulthood that nobody prepares you for.

If you’ve ever Googled “is it me?” at 11pm — this is your place.

New episodes fortnightly. Follow so you never miss one.

title: Weighing In: Industrial Scale Stories
author: Edmund Thornefield
description:
Weighing In: Industrial Scale Stories is a practical, industry-focused podcast built for warehouse teams, factory managers, logistics operators, and business owners who depend on precision every day. Each episode explores how industrial weighing supports accuracy, compliance, productivity, and smooth operations across demanding environments.

The series is inspired by Scales 4u’s industrial platform scale range, which includes heavy-duty solutions designed for high-capacity, repeatable weighing in factories, warehouses, and processing facilities. It also reflects the variety of models and features shown on the Scales4U industrial platform scales page, including robust designs, tare functions, RS232 connectivity, stainless steel options, and platform sizes up to 1.5m x 1.5m with capacities up to 3 tons on several models.Midway through the series, listeners will hear expert guidance on choosing the right equipment, including the micro a12e scale, and how the right platform scale can improve workflow, reduce costly errors, and support safer handling in industrial settings.

The goal of the show is to turn technical weighing knowledge into clear, useful insights that help listeners make smarter equipment decisions and get more value from their operations.

We explore topics such as addiction & recovery, the “success wound”, grief, LGBTQIA+ issues, chronic pain, the effects of childhood Trauma, anxiety and depression, postpartum mood disorders, disorderly eating and many more salient topics. title: The Humble Hang
author: Ritesh Patel
description:
Most business podcasts talk to the already-famous.

The Humble Hang goes looking for the ones nobody has found yet. Every week, the show sits down with a misfit, a maker, or a quiet revolutionary — someone in healthcare, technology, or somewhere in between — who decided the world as it existed was not good enough.

Not a complaint. A blueprint. No panels. No platitudes. No breathless coverage of the last funding round. Just an honest conversation about what it takes to build something that matters, from people who are actually doing it.

The guests are different every week. The question is always the same: what do you see that everyone else is missing? Some are building new tools to fix a broken system. Some are tearing the system down entirely. A few are not sure yet. That uncertainty is exactly where the best ideas live.

This is not a show about success. It is a show about the work before success — the stubborn, unglamorous, occasionally ridiculous work of people who cannot stop themselves from trying. Come as you are. Hang for a while.

Shiroman explores the path to deeper self-connection through inspired writing, philosophical insight, and transformative conversations designed to elevate everyday life. Rooted in the three pillars — Mind, Body, and Soul — this podcast helps listeners reconnect with their authentic selves, embrace healthy masculinity and cultivate a more meaningful, fulfilling existence.
Blending practical skills with mental, physical, and spiritual growth, each episode offers guidance toward living with greater balance, clarity, intention, and alignment.

title: Lifting Up Jesus
author: Minister Eunice Trotter
description:
I started Lifting Up Jesus because I really believe that fear of dying comes from not knowing what is waiting on the other side, and I refuse to let people stay in that darkness one more day. Every week, I bring you faith-grounded truth, real conversations, and the kind of clarity that moves you from frozen to free, because my mission is to share the name of Jesus with every soul who is searching for God, and does not yet know He is already drawing them close. If you have ever laid awake wondering where you will go when you die, I built this show for you, and you need it in your life right now.
You are not broken for being afraid. You just have not heard the truth yet, and that is exactly why I am here.
My mission is to take you from anxious and uncertain to calm, clear, and ready to act on your faith, because understanding what comes next changes how you live today.
Life is filled with noise that cancels out the truth you need to hear. I used to be that person who desperately needed to hear truth at a time when my life had fallen apart, and the feeling of hopelessness consumed me; paralyzing my every effort. This podcast was built for those who can relate to the feeling of being stuck, and afraid to make the next move. It is for those who afraid of dying because you don't know what comes next. I am here to help you experience your God given freedom.

title: Let’s Get Publishing! How the f*** does the book industry actually work?
author: Sara Aristizabal
description: What I want is for the podcast to give insight into the behind the scenes of book production and distribution. Hopefully, this will become your first step into understanding how books get published and sold to the public. I will be meeting with a variety of people working in or around the book publishing field for a short conversation that will allow us a glimpse into their role in the book world.

title: Hold On, Let Me Save | A Retro and Indie Game Podcast
author: Hold On, Let Me Save
description: From beloved retro console classics to some of the best indie games on PC, FlannelKat and TheLetterTen explore the full spectrum of gaming. Whether the game is a household name or a total mystery to you, a fun conversation is always guaranteed.

title: FASHUN PULL UP
author: Norris Dánta Ford
description:
The business of fashion through the people shaping culture.


Hosted by Norris D. Ford, FASHUN PULL UP explores the stories behind the brands, ideas, and people moving fashion forward. Through conversations with industry leaders and creative innovators, each episode pulls back the curtain on the business, creativity, and culture that most people never get to see.

title: The Religions for Peace Podcast
author: Religions for Peace
description:
Reclaiming the Sacred in a Materialistic World
Policy alone cannot heal a wounded human family. Join us as we explore Shared Sacred Flourishing—a framework for international development that rejects reductionist worldviews and explicitly anchors global peacebuilding, environmental restoration, and human rights in our diverse, resonant experiences of the Sacred.

title: Podcast sobre o poema Still I Rise
author: Rosa Tauane Ramos Santos
description: O poema Still I Rise fala sobre uma mulher forte.

title: Finding My Line Podcast
author: Markus Boschman
description:
A Horror Podcast exploring the depths of fright, terror, and much more. Hosted by Markus Boschman, horror sub-expert, who does not know when he is truly scared in a movie. Explore the depths of the genre with him, and see where it scares you.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Hunter of the Setting Sun
author: N. N. Shepherd
description: Hunter of the Setting Sun is a serialized drama written by N. N. Shepherd. The book follows the story of Emmond , a man seeking the death of the one who killed his wife. Emmond must hunt the killer through terrible magic and strange worlds, seeking justice and revenge. Blending elements of fantasy and science fiction, with dramatic character work and well paced intrigue, Hunter of the Setting Sun is a story worth following.

title: Nevermind...I'll ask myself
author: Darcy Skye
description:
A show for soul-led creatives, sensitives and entrepreneurs who crave stronger connection with their intuition. Explore creative, adventurous ways to work with money, business and heart energy, letting what you build reflect the light of inspiration shining inside you. 
Join Darcy Skye, intuitive guide, entrepreneur, and longtime student of what happens when we learn to trust ourselves.
If you've been puzzling over money, business, creativity, or life for a long time, this show offers a different approach. Instead of standing outside these experiences trying to figure them out, you'll learn how to be in relationship with them.
Through conversations with intuition, money, business, creativity, and the heart, you'll explore new ways of listening, creating, and moving forward. Because when relationship is present, understanding has a way of emerging. Ideas become clearer. Decisions become easier. And what you're building begins to feel more like your own.
Come on inside. Bring your curiosity and sense of adventure. The conversation may be closer than you think.
Stay in touch and close to the action by becoming part of Darcy's email community.  
Go to darcyskye.com to sign up so you're the first to know about new episodes, programs and exciting updates.

title: Ordinary and Remarkable
author: Melanie Rigney
description: In this Rejoice Be Glad podcast, Melanie Rigney explores the lives of people who had an encounter with Jesus and what they can teach us today.

title: Bet on Yourself
author: Darya Topić
description:
If you know you’re meant for more but feel stuck between who you’ve been and who you’re becoming, you’re in the right place.
Hosted by Darya Topic - Peak Performance Coach, Speaker, former professional athlete, and founder of The Bet On Yourself Method.
Each week, Darya shares honest conversations on self-trust, courage, identity, and what it really takes to stop settling & go all in on yourself - creating a life that feels fully yours.
Work with Darya: www.iamdaryatopic.com

title: I'll Go If You Go
author: Dear Media
description: Stef Dag & Gabby Bryan are best friends/touring comedians with severe fomo and also a crippling desire to lay in bed and stare into the void. They’ve traveled the globe together and have developed an ethos of best friendship centered on a willingness to do anything as long as they have the other by their side. Each episode of I’ll Go if You Go takes listeners to a new location (physical or meta): a fashion week party we hated, an internet rabbit hole we found, a trip to Croatia where we doubled our body count… all explorations and celebrations of our decades long friendship that mostly finds us laying back to back in a Marriot Bonvoy on our phones.

title: The Manager Minute
author: Brookfield Private Wealth
description: Welcome to Brookfield Private Wealth’s focused podcast series delivering concise updates on alternative investments. We share market perspectives, as well as investment performance highlights, portfolio insights, and positioning.

title: Storm Tested Leadership
author: James
description:
When it hits the fan it frequently brings out the very best, and occasionally the very worst, in organizations, communities, and individuals. 


This podcast is devoted to delivering the true and impactful stories of those who have weathered significant events or crises and in the process forged valuable leadership lessons for us all.  


Whether their challenges were natural, man-made, accidental or intentional, these leaders emerged STORM TESTED.

title: MedTech Unfiltered
author: Corscience
description:
MedTech Unfiltered is a podcast by Corscience for people who design, develop and bring complex medical technologies to market.
We talk about what really matters in modern MedTech development. From regulatory realities and usability challenges to cybersecurity, AI and system complexity.
To help you make better decisions in an increasingly complex environment.

title: Sunflowers In Heaven - In Search of Truth
author: Sharing Divine Truth Recordings
description:
This channel is dedicated to sharing recordings of discussions by Alan John Miller and Mary Luck who claim to be Jesus and Mary Magdalene of the first century. 
Jesus and Mary feel the truth should be free and live by donation so if you enjoy these audios and feel you have benefited from them please consider donating 
https://www.divinetruth.com and clicking on the donate tab. 
For video recordings of other talks on You Tube https://www.youtube.com/@Divinetruthmain
This channel is hosted by Neena Love - sunflowersinheaven101@gmail.com and is not an official Divine Truth site.

title: Towards Wholeness
author: Dan Jennings
description: Towards Wholeness explores the intersection of psychology, theology, and spirituality. In a fragmented world, we inherit stories that shape our identity, faith, and mental health—for better or worse. This podcast creates space to question those narratives, deconstruct stigma, and explore truth in community. Through conversation, reflection, and thoughtful inquiry, we seek a more integrated way of being where belief, belonging, and wellbeing reinforce one another.

title: No Dams
author: Creek Media
description: Have you ever been curious behind the mic at The Creek radio over the last 10 years?!  We are about to spill it all.  No FCC rules apply!  No stories are safe... this is as candid as it gets.  NO DAMS given.  Designed for true fans of the station and supporters that we'd like to offer something special to.

title: The Happily Ever After Place - A Romance Podcast
author: Pearl and Honey Bookclub
description:
A podcast created for the celebration of the romance genre. We discuss romance novels, authors, readers, films, adaptations, and everything in between. Let’s talk spicy books, tropes, bookish drama, and everything else.
We are “The Happily Ever After” place.
A Pearl and Honey Bookclub podcast

title: Planet Bollywood
author: Tarana
description:
Saagar is a well-known name in the Indian broadcasting world, recognised not only for his music selection but also for the depth of knowledge he brings about the stories behind films and the industry.
 
In the very first episode of Planet Bollywood, he explores the iconic film Kuch Kuch Hota Hai and shares engaging behind-the-scenes insights that Bollywood enthusiasts will appreciate.
 
He reveals how Rani Mukerji was eventually cast in the film, along with interesting details about the actors who were initially considered for Aman’s role. The episode also uncovers where the movie was shot and the reasoning behind those location choices, offering a fresh perspective on a much-loved classic.
 
If you follow Bollywood and enjoy learning about the film industry beyond the screen, this podcast is well worth listening to.

title: KTAO AM 61 PODCAST
author: KTAO AM 61
description: Tune in for regular (pod) Broadcasts from high atop Mt. Olympus in the Pacific NW. On loan from KARC in Tighna AK we welcome our DJ Host Mike Thunder! We feature the musical compositions of an anonymous artist of 55 years known only as The Composer.

title: Rádio globo (não oficial)
author: Thiallyson Assis
description: A versão não oficial da rádio globo. E ele mostra o que não passou lá até os audio

title: SprinklerDude's Podcast
author: Justin Richards
description: Welcome to the SprinklerDude’s Podcast, hosted by Justin Richards. Each episode features real conversations with irrigation professionals, manufacturers, contractors, and industry leaders covering irrigation, outdoor lighting, water conservation, technology, troubleshooting, product reviews, and business growth. Whether you’re a seasoned pro or just getting started, you’ll gain practical insights, expert advice, and real-world solutions. Educate. Empower. Elevate. Live from Studio H2O.

title: Be Au Sm At Fm
author: Joshua Peach
description:
The Be Au Sm (Be Awesome) at FM (Facility Management) is a podcast for Facility Professionals or anyone in the Facility Management space focused on Education Institutions. We will have guests consisting of Facility Professionals in the field, Thought Leaders, Companies that support Education Institutions with services and solutions, and Associations sharing their professional development opportunities.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Heartspace with fifi's Podcast
author: Heartspace with fifi
description: Heartspace with Fifi is a place for honest conversations about life, relationships, personal growth, and everything in between. Every episode is a chance to slow down, reflect, and see life from a different perspective. If you’re looking for a space that feels real, thoughtful, and comforting, you’ve found it. Welcome to Heartspace.

title: History with Wilson: America at 250
author: kylewilson85
description: America at 250 pairs a history buff with a curious novice to unpack the people, fights, and ideas that built America from colonization to today. Each episode aims at real conversation: the kind where one friend asks "wait, what?" and the other actually explains it. Smart, funny, and built for listeners who want to learn more just in time for America's 250th birthday.

title: Navigating life
author: Learning life
description: Just a girl who has big dreams however unsure about how to navigate life so let’s do it together for the love ,laugh and whatever life throws at us‼️

title: Shine with Carrie Podcast
author: Carrie Rene Thomas
description:
Everybody liked me. Nobody really knew me. This podcast is me changing that.
I'm Carrie Rene Thomas. I've spent years helping other women get seen, and this is me finally taking my own advice. Each episode, I answer one honest question out loud, about the woman I'm becoming, the things I'm building, my faith, my brain, and my delightfully delulu goals. Just me, my tea, a good mocktail, or my favorite Waterloo.
It's about me, but it's for you. If watching me go first gives you permission to take the step you've been sitting on, that's the whole point. Pull up a chair. Shine easy.

title: Introduction to the Mission
author: sofieldhealth
description:
At Sofield Health, we transform science into results that matter... Here is an overview of what we do as an organization.

Cover art photo provided by Planet Volumes on Unsplash: https://unsplash.com/@planetvolumes?utm_source=spreaker&utm_medium=referral

title: 2 Unhinged
author: Kathleen
description:
Stories, laughs + life in the third act

Because ageing doesn’t dim you — it polishes you!

Hosted by Kathy and Mark

title: Unapologetically Indulgent
author: LaGail Michelle
description:
This is a space for the woman in transition. The one reclaiming herself, piece by piece, after years of putting everyone else first.


Here, we talk about self-devotion like it's sacred, because it is. We indulge the senses without apology. We slow down, we feel deeply, and we stop asking permission to want what we want.


LaGail Michelle brings you real conversations about healing, becoming, and choosing yourself on purpose. This isn't self-help lite. It's an invitation to come home to your own body, your own desires, and your own truth, unapologetically.

If you're tired of shrinking, this show is for you.

title: Fr. Moore’s Pastor’s Notes
author: Rev. Jeffrey Moore
description:
Fr. Moore’s weekly Pastor’s Notes, read for your convenience.
Fr. Moore is Pastor of Family #2 (Whatcom County) of the Archdiocese of Seattle.

title: The Transformative Divorce
author: Ezty Rosynfeld
description:
Divorce is often called one of life's most stressful experiences, second only to the death of a loved one - but it doesn't have to end in bitterness and stagnation.  Ezty Rosynfeld, divorce attorney and coach, explores how the pain of separation can become the catalyst for a stronger, more purposeful life.


Drawing on real client stories and hands-on tools developed through decades of guiding people through custody battles, contested settlements, and emotional upheaval, each episode digs into a different piece of the transformation process: reframing your story instead of being trapped by it, overcoming resistance and procrastination, breaking out of old patterns and comfort zones, rebuilding physically, socially, and spiritually, and ultimately designing a vision for the next chapter of life.


This isn't a show about staying stuck in anger or seeking revenge through settlements or custody fights. It's about learning to view your divorce, and the events that led to it,  as exactly what needed to happen, and using that shift in perspective to catapult yourself into a new life built with confidence, creativity, and gratitude.


Whether you're in the middle of a divorce, processing an old one, or supporting someone who is, The Transformative Divorce offers practical exercises and a compassionate, experience-based roadmap for turning heartbreak into growth.

title: Squirrel AT MY THROAT
author: Tanja
description:
Squirrel At My Throat is based on my book by the same name.  The subtitle is "Living and Dying Unconventionally in a Conventional World".  My book is a memoir, primarily devoted to portraying my son's cancer experience, but I raise so many other topics in the course of the book which I feel merit much more discussion as well as examination from a wide variety of perspectives, including the importance of family, how young adults deal with cancer, the state of cancer treatment, mental health, the afterlife, cancel culture, drugs, the state of education, grief, bereaved parenting, the flaws of many societal institutions, music, raising boys, the importance of family, medical freedom, vaccination, climate change, digital currency, digital ID, transgenderism etc. etc. etc.  Many of the topics that I discuss will emerge organically from the current issues of the day as well as from the content of my book.  My book is available two ways:

https://books.friesenpress.com/store/title/119734000137473637/Tanja-Zientara-Squirrel-At-My-Throat or https://www.amazon.ca/portal/customer-reviews/1525581252/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_show_all_top?_encoding=UTF8&ie=UTF8&reviewerType=all_reviews

By the way, the opening song is titled, "Me, You and Labatt Blue", written and performed by my deceased son and his band.

title: The Rusty Nail - "Habits of Mind"
author: Rob Norwood
description: Habits of Mind is a podcast that exploes ways of thinking and achieving goals and success.  Thought provoking and insightful this podcast creates paths for deep thinking.

title: Rhys Trueman's Podcast
author: Rhys Trueman
description: POSTPONED  Unfortunately due to the wet weather our Kids Bike Maintenance Session at Selwyn Ridge School today has been postponed. Keep posted for the new date these school holidays!

title: The Harvest Book one Podcast
author: Sown Publishing
description:
This is the audiobook of my novel "The Harvest" each chapter will be released weekly. She wakes strapped to a chair in a room of blinding white—with no name, no past, only 349, spoken by a voice too smooth to be human. Each time the machines dig into her mind, flashes break through the void: blood, a child’s laugh, arms holding her tight. Memories she’s not supposed to have.

They call it interference. She knows it’s the truth trying to surface.

When the mask lowers again and cold floods her skull, the lights tremble like the room itself is bracing for impact. Just before everything snaps to black, one realization cuts through the fear—a truth she can’t unhear or escape.

They’re not repairing her. They’re rewriting her.

title: Aline GF4
author: Taka Kota
description: Aline GF4

title: Learn On Twitch Podcast
author: Ufia Sfyat
description:
Join me for exciting English challenges, fun Guesswork adventures, and full Twitch stream replays. Whether you're here to improve your English, explore the world through Guesswork,

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/learn-on-twitch-podcast--7158150/support.

title: Beyond the Backyard | Hardscaping Stitts
author: Ethan Robinson
description:
Outdoor spaces should do more than look attractive—they should be built to perform for years.

This podcast explores the planning, materials, and craftsmanship behind premium landscape construction projects. From interlock patios and retaining walls to walkways, driveways, and custom outdoor living spaces, every discussion highlights practical ideas that help property owners make confident decisions before starting their next project.

If you're researching hardscaping stittsville, you'll find valuable insights on durable materials, thoughtful design, and proven installation techniques that enhance both curb appeal and long-term property value. Each episode blends real landscaping experience with practical advice to inspire outdoor spaces that are as functional as they are beautiful.

title: Tracks That Matter
author: Nicole Morrison
description:
A song comes on, and suddenly you're somewhere else.You're seventeen again. Driving with the windows down. Sitting beside someone you miss. Surviving something you weren't sure you'd make it through. Falling in love. Finding yourself.

On Tracks That Matter, Nicole M. Morrison explores the songs that become woven into our lives and the stories attached to them. Each guest brings a track that matters, and together they uncover the memories, lessons, heartbreaks, victories, and defining moments connected to it.

What begins as a conversation about music often becomes something deeper: a conversation about what it means to be human.

Because long after the song ends, the story remains.

And every story leaves a track.

Have a song & a story that matters to you? Share it at TracksThatMatter.com

title: Woo With Proof
author: LindsayRose
description:
Woo with Proof is the podcast for people who love the woo but want the proof.
Hosted by Lindsay Rose, this show explores the intersection of spirituality, science, psychology, manifestation, consciousness, and human potential. Through thought-provoking conversations and deep dives into both mystical experiences and measurable mechanisms, you'll discover what happens when intuition meets evidence and belief meets understanding.
Because the most interesting questions in life live somewhere between magic and science.

title: Jusga Muna
author: Ichigo Kuro
description: Jusga Muna

title: The Good Love with Steph & Jonah
author: Steph Campbell
description:
The Good Love with Steph & Jonah is a podcast about the beautiful mess of being human.
Hosted by mother and son Steph and Jonah, each week features honest conversations about the experiences, beliefs, and relationships that shape who we become. Together, they explore the stories we inherit, the wounds we carry, and the hope that comes from learning to see ourselves—and each other—with greater compassion.
Blending personal stories, thoughtful reflection, psychological insight, and a healthy dose of humor, Steph and Jonah tackle life’s biggest questions with curiosity instead of certainty. From relationships and parenting to healing, mental health, purpose, and personal growth, every episode invites listeners to think more deeply, love more freely, and live more intentionally.
Whether you’re navigating a difficult season or simply looking for meaningful conversations that remind you you’re not alone, The Good Love with Steph & Jonah offers a place to slow down, reflect, and grow.
Because a better relationship with yourself changes every relationship that follows.

title: Waders: Prairie Water Stories
author: Dominique Ferraton
description:
What does it mean to care for water in the semi-arid region of Southwestern Saskatchewan?
We journey to shallow wetlands, spring-fed lakes and drought-stricken farms. We listen to farmers, scientists, activists and naturalists. Weaving together natural and cultural history, this series considers how we might start to repair our relationship with water here in the driest part of Canada.
Waders: Prairie Water Stories is produced by Dominique Ferraton. Associate producer is Magdalena Nichol. Original music is by B-Sides Family Publishing House and Ian Ross. Thank you to SK Arts for financial support. For resources and references visit waderspodcast.ca

title: Zombie: Year One
author: Jeremy Elice
description:
From Jeremy Elice — the AMC executive who brought The Walking Dead and Breaking Bad to television.
The zombie apocalypse has been told a thousand times. This is the one story no one has ever told: Day One.
Zombie: Year One is a weekly anthology series. Each episode drops you into a different location — a restaurant on the Sunset Strip, a protest, a drag race, a red-eye flight, a house party — as an outbreak tears through an unsuspecting America. Every story stands alone. Together they form a portrait of society's collapse from the ground up.
The outbreak hits in two waves. The first looks like illness. The second looks like the end of the world.
New episode every Sunday at 8pm.

title: The Kakuzu
author: Abelardo Jonas
description: The Kakuzu

title: The Daily Buzz
author: Br Zuca
description: The Daily Buzz

title: Faith Unscripted
author: Monica
description:
On this podcast, we aren't chasing perfection, and we definitely aren't pretending to have all the answers. This is just a raw, honest look at what it means to grow in relationship with God.


​We're going to dive into the scriptures, talk about the real-life struggles of faith, and honestly, just share whatever it is that God puts on my heart to give to you each week. Think of it as a weekly sit-down with a friend who is right there in the trenches of growth with you.


So, whether you are in a season of waiting, a season of celebrating, or you're just trying to figure out what God is doing in your life right now.......you belong here!

title: The Doctrine Drop
author: Steven Pray
description: The Doctrine Drop Focuses on turning gospel doctrine into daily action. Our goal is to learn gospel principles and practical ways to put those principles into action.

title: How To Become a Musician
author: Krissy Music
description:
Do you want to become musician? Discover new indie artists sharing their stories of how they started doing music, from learning instruments to songwriting to promoting their music on social media. Whether you want to pursue music as a side gig, a full-time career, or just as a hobby, this podcast is for you!
Host Krissy is a singer/songwriter, working as a script editor with a background in marketing and SEO, sharing insights as she releases original music.
Catch weekly episodes for international interviews with independent artists sharing strategies to grow as a musician!

title: Late Night Talks by Aphryllah
author: Code59
description:
Late Night Talks with Aphryllah

When the world grows quiet, the heart finally finds its voice.

Late Night Talks with Aphryllah is a cozy space for honest conversations about love, healing, patience, self-worth, hope, and life's beautiful uncertainties. Through heartfelt stories, gentle reflections, and comforting words, each episode is here to remind you that you're never alone in what you feel.

Whether you're falling in love, healing from heartbreak, chasing your dreams, or simply searching for a little peace before you sleep, pull up a chair, take a deep breath, and let's talk.

Because sometimes, the conversations we need the most happen late at night.

title: Build What Lasts
author: Peter Tardiff
description: Build what lasts is about Catholic culture, the skilled labor, and the intellectual life. On it, professors from San Damiano College for the Trades and special guests reflect on the mutually enriching relationship between the liberal arts and the trades in a Catholic context.

title: Quickly Quantum with Brian Lampert
author: Brian Lampert
description: Quantum computing is about to change everything — stay ahead of it in 10 minutes a day. Every weekday, Quickly Quantum cuts through the hype to bring you the breakthroughs, funding rounds, policy moves, and research that actually matter, with plain-English analysis a smart non-physicist can follow. Hosted by Brian Lampert. AI-voiced, human-built, always skeptical of press releases. Nothing on this show is financial advice.

title: Fr Patrick Cahill
author: Called to More
description: This is a weekly video series by Called to More in collaboration with Holy Family Mission. Fr Patrick Cahill brings you quality Catholic content with new episodes every Tuesday at 9pm Irish time (4pm Eastern / 1pm Pacific).

title: Atlas of Presence
author: Theophanergia Teams
description: THEOPHANERGIA is a contemplative podcast exploring the architecture of Presence and the mystery of existence. Each episode gently guides you from everyday fragmentation toward deeper insight, offering unique perspectives on consciousness, meaning, and being. This is a journey beyond self-help—an invitation to deep awareness and ontological discovery.

title: Not Managed. Fully Lived.
author: Michele Gray (Oelking)
description:
You have done the work. The strategies, the protocols, the mindset shifts. You keep hitting a ceiling you cannot fully explain.


This show is for you.


Not Managed, Fully Lived. is a podcast for high-achieving, neurodivergent women who are ready to go beneath the executive function conversation and into the biological terrain nobody in the ADHD space has addressed at the level it deserves. Long-form. Personal essay. The kind of conversation that treats your intelligence as the given it always has been.


I am Michele Gray (Oelking), MSW, PCC, AAC, Founder and CEO of Souljourn Coaching & Consulting. For 26 years, I've practiced at the intersection of ADHD, trauma, and the nervous system. I call myself the Neurosomatic Alchemist, because this work resides between applied neuroscience, positive psychology, ancestral wisdom, and embodied practice.


Each episode goes to the 80 percent. The nervous system layer. The somatic truth. The biological terrain that determines how you show up to every strategy you have ever tried. You will hear research and personal essay, lived experience and clinical framework, held in the same breath.


Episodes drop weekly from seven acres in the mountains of Colorado.


Not managed. Fully lived.

title: FindingMyQua
author: Qua
description: Welcome to FindingMyQua—a space where real life meets real conversation. This podcast is a candid, empowering journey through life, love, and the realities of navigating corporate America as a person of color, all while balancing the beautiful chaos of single motherhood.

title: Potenza Capital Weekly Recaps
author: Potenza Capital
description: Weekly recaps on everything Potenza Capital

title: Larry Brock's The Reckoning Shorts
author: Larry Brock
description:
Welcome to The Reckoning Podcast, I’m Larry Brock, a retired US Air Force Lieutenant Colonel who flew A-10xs in Iraq and Afghanistan, and a native Texan fighting to take our state back from career politicians.

They tried to silence me, but we are just getting started. This channel is the home for conservative Texans who are tired of weak leadership and broken promises. We cover the real battles happening in Austin and Washington: destroying the property tax system, stopping the radical left, halting globalist influence, and putting Texas citizens first.


Subscribe for regular video updates, legislative breakdowns, and direct commentary you won't find anywhere else. > 


Join the Fight: Hit SUBSCRIBE and turn on notifications so you never miss a video.

Watch live: Facebook, X, and YouTube weekdays at 11am CST

title: fraser's Podcast
author: fraser
description: fraser's podcast is about chats with hair colourists.

title: The Outskirts
author: Leigh Antill
description:
The Outskirts is a podcast of personal essays and conversations with women writers who are writing about the more-than-human world.
Episodes move between intimate narrative essays and slow, honest dialogue—about land, work, creativity, and the complicated terrain of being a woman who writes. These are conversations that don’t rush toward answers, but stay close to the questions we live inside.
Part essay, part interview, The Outskirts lives where language meets landscape—listening for what becomes possible when women write from the margins, and name what they find there.

title: Rooted In Resilience Podcast
author: Zarina Christian
description: Rooted in Resilience is a podcast about navigating adversity, healing, and transformation. Hosted by Zarina Christian — entrepreneur, mother, and resilience advocate — each episode is an honest conversation about the real experiences that shape us into stronger, wiser versions of ourselves. Topics include relationships, entrepreneurship, mental wellness, heartbreak, leadership, and self-reinvention. New episodes weekly. Subscribe and join a community of people choosing to grow through what they go through.

title: קול "מתנ"ס הצעירים" - ערוץ הסכתים קהילתי
author: radio kids
description: ערוץ הפודקאסט הרשמי של מתנ"ס הצעירים

title: Practice Makes The Master
author: Arn Terry
description:
Mastery isn't a destination. It's a way of living. Practice Makes The Master is for people who refuse to drift through life and choose to become architects of their own future. 
Hosted by Arn Terry, each episode explores timeless principles of self-mastery, psychology, business, wealth, leadership, spirituality, and the extraordinary power of consistent action. Through thought-provoking conversations, practical wisdom, and powerful stories, you'll discover that success isn't built in giant leaps—it's created through the small, intentional practices repeated that compound over time.

title: The Dogcast
author: VitaDog Nutrition
description: Real conversations with the people who know dogs best.

title: Inside the Issue
author: Ruby Tocker
description: Join the 2026 Nexus editorial team, Toby, Dylan, Lans, and Ruby, as they jump inside the issue and dive deeper into your favourite student magazine. From BTS moments to takes on each issues' hottest topics to a questionable rant or two, Inside the Issue is guaranteed to satisfy all your Nexus bonus content cravings while also making you ask why we put these four on payroll.

title: BLOC Presents: The BLOC Party Podcast
author: BLOC - Biopharma Leaders of Color
description: Welcome to The BLOC Party Podcast — the official podcast of Biopharma Leaders of Color (BLOC), and a space for candid conversations with leaders shaping the future of biopharma. Each episode brings you behind the scenes on their pathways to success, featuring the wins, the losses, and everything in between. We sit down with heavy hitters to explore their unfiltered insights and lived experiences as they've shaped the industry from the inside and help open doors for those who have historically been left out of the room.

title: Waypoint Camp
author: Waypoint Camp
description: Welcome to the Camp Sermons Podcast, where you can revisit the messages shared during our summer camp. Whether you were there in person or are listening for the first time, our prayer is that these sermons encourage you, strengthen your faith, and point you closer to Christ.

title: Project Maximus
author: Max Barnes
description:
Project Maximus is a mission dedicated to unlocking the greatest version of yourself. I'm Max Barnes and I built Project Maximus inside 80-hour work weeks and a 120-mile daily commute without waiting for ideal conditions. Every episode digs into the methodology and mythology extracted from years of excavating who I am, how I operate, and what I'm capable of. Forged inside a hectic life. Ain't nothin' to it but to do it. Welcome to Project Maximus! Walk with me.
Check out more at https://project-maximus.com

title: Murder Mystery Crime Podcast
author: Ted
description: Murder Mystery Weekly Crime  Podcast.

title: Butter Brothers
author: Misa & Fabi
description: Hey Butters! Join us every episode for random moments, deep talks, and everything in between. Just two guys being two guys on a podcast.

title: Abion Accento
author: Abion
description: Real IP stories from the people who lead it. Join Lorenzo Litta, Chief Business Officer at Abion, in conversations with IP leaders on brands, business, and the decisions that shape them.

title: The Smarter Supply Chain: AI for Procurement
author: Carlo Selorio
description:
Clear thinking for supply chain leaders navigating AI.


Hosted by Carlo, a procurement and supply chain practitioner with 28 years in the field, The Smarter Supply Chain cuts through the vendor hype to answer the only question that matters: is this tool taking your time or your judgment? No breathless futurism, no sales decks — just honest, practitioner-led takes on the AI tools actually changing how we work, including the failures alongside the wins. If you do this work, or lead people who do, this show is built for you.

title: The Plastics
author: Coalr LLC
description: The internet’s a busy place, but you’re busy too. The Plastics pod is here to keep you in the loop with quick roundups from across internet culture and entertainment every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: The Coordination Tax
author: In Parallel
description: This is the Coordination Tax, a podcast about what actually breaks down when teams stop sharing the same reality. This is the coordination tax, the show that is about what actually breaks down when teams stop sharing the same reality.

title: The Everyday Edit
author: favour
description:
The Everyday Edit is my little corner of the internet where we slow down, talk it out, and get curious together.
Each week, I share my honest take on beauty, fashion, internet culture, wellness, shopping finds, and the routines that shape everyday life. It's not about having all the answers, it's about having real conversations and looking at the bigger picture beyond the headlines, trends, or viral moments.
If you love thoughtful commentary, cozy chats, and staying in the loop without the noise, you're in the right place.💕

title: The Favor Experience with Suprina Brown
author: The Favor Experience
description: Welcome to The Favor Experience with Suprina Brown, where faith, favor, and purpose come together through real conversations and life-changing insights. Each episode is designed to inspire, encourage, and equip you to overcome challenges, walk confidently in your calling, and embrace God's best for your life. You'll hear powerful testimonies, practical wisdom, and uplifting discussions that leave you motivated, empowered, and ready for what's next. If you're looking for hope, growth, and inspiration, this podcast is for you.

title: Here and Calm
author: Here and Calm
description: Here and Calm offers simple guided meditations that help you meet your mind, body, and emotions with curiosity and kindness. Each episode introduces a different style of practice—like mindfulness, loving‑kindness, body scans, walking, or mantra—so you can gently explore what works best for you and bring a little more stillness into everyday life.

title: Fantasy Free Me - Fabulous & Fantastical Fiction
author: Amy Marsh
description:
A Podcast for Writers, Readers, & Advocates of Queer & Progressive Fantasy Fiction.


With guests drawn from a plucky band of marginalized writers, publishers, readers, and advocates for queer and progressive fantasy fiction, Fantasy Free Me is one writer's quest to right the wrongs of an evil empire of literary oligarchs and uplifts the voices of people driven to world-build alternatives where we all can thrive as creators and readers.


Podcast host A.R. Marsh is the author of The Guild of Ornamental Hermits contemporary fantasy series.


Theme Music Attribution Code:

"Suonatore di Liuto" Kevin MacLeod (incompetech.com)

Licensed under Creative Commons: By Attribution 4.0 License

http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Speciality Coffee Podcast
author: Storyline Coffee Roasters
description: We interview the biggest names in the speciality coffee industry to learn from the journey through the coffee world.

title: In Bloom with Bri
author: Briana Prickett
description:
A few years ago, I thought building something bold was the assignment.
Then God asked me to lay it down.
Not because it failed…
but because He was calling me deeper first.
So I entered my quiet season.
My mom era.
My hidden season.
I focused on my home.
My family.
My heart.
And somewhere in the stillness, God renewed me.
He reminded me that obedience matters more than visibility.
That blooming happens underground long before anyone sees it.
And now…
He’s calling me back.
Not to build a platform.
But to use my voice for His kingdom.
Welcome to In Bloom with Bri.

title: AI Over 40
author: John C Donahue
description: AI Over 40 is the podcast for professionals, entrepreneurs, and curious minds over 40 who want to understand artificial intelligence without the hype or confusion. Each episode explores practical AI tools, smart strategies, and real-world use cases for business, productivity, creativity, and everyday life. Whether you’re new to AI or ready to level up, this show helps you stay informed, confident, and ahead of the curve in a fast-changing digital world.

title: The Car Guys
author: Sanna Phillips
description:
Buying a car shouldn’t feel like a gamble.

On The Car Concierge Podcast, the team at Car Deal Max pulls back the curtain on the car-buying process, sharing real stories, practical advice, and the expertise they’ve built over years in the automotive industry. From avoiding costly mistakes to finding the right vehicle for your needs, every conversation is designed to help you buy with confidence.

What sets Car Deal Max apart is simple: they stand behind every deal. Even when the easy answer would be to walk away, they choose to do what’s right for their customers. It’s a different kind of buying experience—one built on trust, transparency, and relationships that last long after the keys are handed over.

Whether you’re shopping for your first car, your next family vehicle, or your dream ride, this is where smarter car buying begins.

title: Hollow Hype
author: Elizabeth Nelson
description: Ann Arbor, Michigan is one of the most educated communities in the country, but its local government was taken in by a twice convicted felon. In California, Heidi went to prison for fraud. In West Virginia, she was convicted of conspiracy to commit money laundering and narrowly avoided prison. Beginning in 2020, elected leaders and City staff in Ann Arbor promoted Heidi's businesses and embraced her as a community partner. Former Ann Arbor City Council Member Elizabeth Nelson shares what happened in Michigan. She digs through local archives and government records to understand how other communities fell victim to HOLLOW HYPE.

title: The China History Podcast
author: Laszlo Montgomery
description: Since 2010, The China History Podcast, presented by Laszlo Montgomery, has brought you hundreds of episodes of curated historical topics from China's antiquity to modern times.

title: Navigating the Maize
author: Sean
description: A weekly, 15-minute podcast on nonprofits and AI — real workflows, no hype, hosted by a 30-year nonprofit development veteran.

title: Post-Concussion Resilience Podcast
author: David Brimacombe
description:
The Post-Concussion Resilience Podcast is for people living with persistent post-concussion syndrome, PCS, chronic PCS, and ongoing concussion symptoms who feel trapped, unsupported, or limited.
Hosted by David Brimacombe, this podcast explores lifestyle-based recovery systems for nervous system regulation, sleep, movement, mindset, nutrition, identity, and self-trust.
This is not about quick fixes. It is about becoming an active participant in your healing.
You are not broken. You are rebuilding.

title: The 7 with Scottie
author: Scottie
description: Welcome to the official premiere of The 7 with Scottie! We’re kicking things off with a bang—and absolutely zero warning.​In this debut episode, we aren’t following a script. We caught up with KO Kelly for a phone interview, but there was one major catch: they had no idea we were calling. Tune in to hear the unfiltered, candid, and completely spontaneous conversation that happens when the phone rings and the mic is already hot.​This isn’t your average pre-recorded sit-down. It’s raw, it’s unexpected, and it’s the perfect way to launch the The 7 experience. If you’re looking for genuine, unscripted content, you’ve found the right frequency.

title: Ripple Makers
author: Charles Davis & Susie Cowan
description: This series would share the stories of Macon / Central Georgia non-profits giving them a platform to raise awareness of their cause and share the role they play in the Macon community.

title: People Behind the Power
author: Metric DCX
description: Data centres are the backbone of the digital world. But behind every facility, every build and every operational decision, there are people making it happen. Every episode we sit down with the leaders driving the data centre industry forward. The careers they've built, the teams they've scaled are what it actually takes to deliver at the pace this sector demands.

title: WeAreDevelopers TechRecruiting Podcast
author: WeAreDevelopers
description: Welcome to WeAreDevelopers TechRecruiting Podcast, where we explore ways to improve tech candidate experience, imagine the future of tech recruitment, and present the best practices with the talent acquisition professionals that are winning the war for talent! Our guests are HR leaders, recruiting professionals, and game-changers from all industries and subject areas. Podcast episodes are in English or German language.

title: Shoes Off Talk
author: Floyd Ellis, Tim McAfee, Kristy Austin, Larvell S. Whitt
description:
Shoes Off Talk is where real conversations begin. We take off the masks, leave the titles at the door, and dive into honest, unfiltered discussions about life, culture, relationships, business, faith, current events, personal growth, and everything in between.
Hosted with authenticity and respect, Shoes Off Talk creates a space where every guest has the opportunity to share their journey, their struggles, and their victories. Whether you're looking for inspiration, motivation, laughter, or thought-provoking dialogue, you'll find conversations that challenge perspectives and encourage meaningful connections.
We believe that the best conversations happen when people are comfortable enough to be themselves because when the shoes come off, the real talk begins.
Unapologetic Conversations. Where Real Conversations Live.

title: Did You Hear Tho? – Celebrity Gossip and Pop Culture
author: Pop-i
description:
Did You Hear Tho? – Celebrity Gossip and Pop Culture is your go-to podcast for the latest celebrity gossip, trending entertainment news, viral moments, and everything happening in the world of pop culture. 


From Hollywood headlines and music industry buzz to social media drama, reality TV, fashion, movies, and unforgettable celebrity moments, we break down the stories everyone is talking about.

Whether it's the biggest celebrity breakups, surprise engagements, chart-topping music releases, blockbuster films, award shows, or internet-shaking controversies, Did You Hear Tho? delivers entertaining commentary, insightful discussions, and fresh takes that keep you informed and entertained.


Tune in each episode to stay ahead of the latest trends, discover behind-the-scenes stories, and join the conversation on the hottest topics shaping today's entertainment world. If you love celebrity news, pop culture commentary, viral internet moments, and staying in the know, this podcast is made for you.


Subscribe now and never miss the next big story—because if everyone's talking about it, you'll hear it here first on Did You Hear Tho?

title: Calvary Church Podcast
author: CHRIS
description: Weekly sermons from Calvary Church in West Lafayette Indiana

title: English Podcast
author: rokon Biswas
description: Welcome to English Art! English Art is a podcast designed to help you improve your English through engaging conversations, practical lessons, and real-life examples. Whether you're a beginner or an advanced learner, each episode will build your vocabulary, strengthen your grammar, improve your pronunciation, and boost your confidence in speaking English. Join us and make learning English simple, enjoyable, and inspiring—one episode at a time.

title: Hinos Da Mensagem
author: Hinos Da Mensagem
description: Musicas Evangélicas

title: English Communication Hub
author: Feen barry
description:
Welcome to English Communication Hub—your go-to podcast for improving your English speaking and communication skills with confidence.
Whether you're a beginner or an advanced learner, each episode is packed with practical English conversations, everyday vocabulary, pronunciation tips, speaking strategies, and communication techniques to help you sound more natural and fluent.
Learn how to speak confidently in real-life situations, job interviews, presentations, business meetings, and daily conversations. Our goal is to make English learning simple, engaging, and useful for your personal, academic, and professional success.

title: VetLaunch Lab: Companion Animal Commercial Intelligence
author: VetLaunch Lab
description: VetLaunch Lab decodes the business side of companion animal health — regulatory moves, product launches, pricing shifts, and pipeline bets — and translates them into what they actually mean for brand and commercial teams. Each episode breaks down the week's signals from across the industry's major players, cutting through the noise to answer the one question that matters: so what does this mean for your strategy? Built for marketing, commercial, and brand leaders at animal health companies who need to stay ahead of the competition, not just informed about it.

title: What the Tech? Season 2
author: ProServeIT
description: Immerse yourself in our What The Tech podcast to discover the limitless possibilities of technology and its transformative impact on your business. Gain valuable insights on how Microsoft technology can propel your company to new heights. Subscribe now and join the forefront of the digital age.

title: Enlighten Up With Christy
author: Christy Dreiling
description: Join Christy on Enlighten Up, a heart-centered podcast dedicated to exploring the many facets of love, self-love, relationships, purpose, and spirituality. Dive into enriching conversations and inspiring stories that will uplift your spirit and illuminate your path. Whether you're seeking guidance on nurturing your relationships, discovering your true purpose, or deepening your spiritual practice, Christy brings wisdom, compassion, and a touch of humor to each episode. Tune in and transform your journey with insightful interviews, practical tips, and heartfelt love

title: Speak Turkish Like Crazy
author: Speak Turkish Like Crazy
description: Speak Turkish like crazy in no time! Improve your grammar and vocabulary with conversations, common phrases, and short stories even in your car.

title: Stateside with Lee
author: Lee Coppin
description: Stateside with Lee follows one expat's journey from Brit to bona fide US citizen — and everything he's learned along the way. Each episode, Lee explores a different corner of America: the small towns, big cities, roadside diners, national parks, and local characters that make up the real country beneath the headlines. No politics, no punditry — just genuine curiosity about the people, food, traditions, and quirks that make the USA what it is. Whether he's chasing down the best barbecue in Texas, learning why Midwesterners say "you betcha," or figuring out what exactly a "block party" is, Lee brings an outsider's eye and an insider's heart to it all. Part travel show, part culture deep-dive, part love letter to his adopted home, Stateside with Lee is for anyone who's ever wondered what America looks like through fresh eyes — and anyone who's made a new country their own.

title: Beautiful Dreamer: An Audio Drama
author: ST
description: Beautiful Dreamer is an audio drama of the life and music of America’s first professional composer, Stephen Foster.

title: The Right Expert With Bob Craig
author: therightexpertwithbobcraig
description: The Right Expert with Bob Craig is a podcast dedicated to exploring the nuances of expert witness selection and its impact on complex litigation. Designed for legal professionals, the show provides valuable insights into the art of matching experts with cases, ensuring effective communication and collaboration. Host Lisa Mosbey, along with experienced guests like Bob Craig, delves into topics such as expert retention, case strategy, and the human elements of legal proceedings. The series aims to equip attorneys with the knowledge and tools needed to enhance their litigation outcomes through strategic expert engagement.

title: Mx JacklyNyx Nyghtmares
author: Amberousis
description: Actual play tabletop gaming with action horror themes, focused on social, environmental, and industrial degradation.

title: Hook Em' and Book Em'
author: ATX Self-Tape Studio
description:
🎙️ Hook Em' & Book Em' - An Acting podcast.
🎭 Helping Actors navigate the world of film and modern audition process so they can succeed.
🌎 Actors from all over the world pull back the curtain on the audition process and discuss what keeps them motivated to keep performing and auditioning in the modern world.
🎥Whether you're a seasoned professional or just starting your acting journey, we deliver the insights, tips, and motivation you need to book your next role. We're here to help you...HOOK EM" & BOOK EM'!
🎬 PRESENTED BY ATX SELF-TAPE
Visit www.atxselftape.com for more!

title: Revenue Rocks
author: Right Revenue
description: Revenue Rocks… brought to you by Right Revenue… a place where we cut through the noise of revenue management and give you practical advice you can actually use

title: Christian Business Leadership | Watchpost Partners
author: Ashley
description: Watchpost Partners | Christian Business & Leadership is a podcast for business owners, entrepreneurs, and leaders who want to honor God through their work. Hosted by Ashley Nutter, each episode combines biblical wisdom with practical leadership and business insights to help you lead with purpose, steward your time, talents, and resources faithfully, and build a business that impacts your community and glorifies Christ. Empowering Leaders. Strengthening Businesses. Impacting Communities.

title: LUCCI
author: Darren Baker
description:
LUCCI is a high-performance podcast about building wealth, discipline, and clarity through systems.
Host Darren Baker breaks down the operating principles behind execution, resilience, health, money, and relationships—without fluff.
Expect strategy you can apply immediately: mental models, personal operating systems, and real-world lessons on building a life with control and impact.
New episodes deliver actionable frameworks—and a challenge to level up your standards.

title: Learn Dutch with Alain
author: Learn Dutch with Alain
description: Learn Dutch with Alain offers Dutch lessons for beginners through advanced learners, including grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation, and exam prep. Visit https://learnanylanguages.com/dutch/private-dutch-lessons/ for lessons and companion resources.

title: MPCC Podcast: A Rural Community College
author: Mid-Plains Community College
description: Mid-Plains Community College, a two-year college in southwestern Nebraska, is more than just a place to learn, it’s a place where journeys begin. This podcast dives into the stories that make our community college unique, from students and faculty to alumni and local voices. Each episode highlights the experiences, opportunities, and connections that show why starting at a community college can be the smartest and most rewarding way to transform your future. Whether you're exploring your options or already part of the MPCC family, join us as we share what truly makes us unique.

title: The Love Jones Experience
